what_it_does: This file is a ledger/hub card for the unsloth Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF Q3_K_M model variant, recording its metadata, selection rationale, runtime path via Ollama, known quirks, open questions, and links to related model entries.
observations: The model file is 15.5 GB and was chosen to fit inside 16 GB VRAM with thin KV margin.
It is a 35B-total / ~3B-active MoE quantized to GGUF Q3_K_M using unsloth's dynamic 'UD' calibration.
It is registered with Ollama as qwen3.6-35b-a3b-q3km:latest and served on port 11434, not vLLM.
The entry explicitly states it has never been load-tested, benchmarked, or coherence-verified.
Ollama's GGUF path is single-process and queues concurrent requests rather than batching them, which may limit throughput.
Tool calling through the Ollama + GGUF path is noted as unverified.
Native context length and whether Ollama loads it fully in GPU VRAM are flagged as unknown.
It links to a lower-bit sibling (IQ3_S), a dense alternative (Qwen3.6-27B), and a competing vLLM-served MoE (Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-AWQ).
standouts: It is positioned as the largest local model by capability tier after the dense 27B, yet it is completely unmeasured.
The entry highlights a tension: the MoE architecture promises 3B-active throughput, but Ollama's non-batching GGUF serving may undercut that advantage.
It documents a deliberate quantization tradeoff (Q3_K_M as the highest-bit variant fitting a 4080) rather than just listing specs.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, ollama_name, date_added, and tags.

Follows a wiki-style ledger structure with an 'At a glance' table, 'Source', 'Fits these needs', 'Tested performance', 'Known-good launch config', 'Quirks', 'Open at this entry', and 'Connects to' sections.
Uses neutral, factual language and explicitly marks unverified claims.
concerns: The file makes performance assumptions (3B-active tok/s, high-throughput chat suitability) without any measured data.
It warns that 15.5 GB into 16 GB VRAM may still spill to CPU, which would invalidate the intended use case.
Tool calling, long-context behavior, and actual Ollama-exposed context length are all unverified but listed as operational considerations.
The date_added value (2026-05-27) is in the future relative to current knowledge, which may be a data-entry artifact.
notes: This is an unmeasured Ollama-hosted 35B/3B-active MoE ledger card that explains why the Q3_K_M variant was selected and clearly flags every operational claim that still needs validation.
questions: What is the actual single-stream tok/s when loaded by Ollama on the target GPU?
Does Ollama keep the full 15.5 GB model in GPU VRAM, or does it spill to system RAM/CPU?
What context length does Ollama actually expose for this model?
Does tool calling work correctly through the Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint for this GGUF file?
How does its output quality compare to qwen3.5-4b-awq on the typical workload?
Is the lower-bit IQ3_S sibling needed in practice, or does Q3_K_M fit reliably?
How does its real throughput compare to the vLLM-served Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-AWQ candidate?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to serve as a model ledger entry; the file matches that purpose by cataloging metadata, rationale, runtime constraints, and open validation items.
apparent_themes: Memory-capability tradeoffs in local model selection.
Quantization as a practical compression strategy with quality caveats.
Runtime path constraints (Ollama vs vLLM) shaping model suitability.
Operational humility: explicitly recording unmeasured claims and unknowns.
MoE throughput expectations versus actual serving bottlenecks.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a decision record for choosing this specific quantized MoE variant, while simultaneously warning the reader not to treat its theoretical advantages as proven until load testing is performed.
novelty: It explicitly foregrounds the risk that Ollama's single-process GGUF serving may cancel out the MoE throughput benefit, and it treats unsloth's dynamic 'UD' quantization as a calibrated choice rather than a default.
contribution: It preserves the exact metadata, selection rationale, runtime constraints, and open validation checklist for this quantized MoE model, giving a fused system concrete, neutral facts to compare against sibling and competitor entries without assuming superiority.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger card for the unsloth Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF Q3_K_M variant, a 15.5 GB 35B-total/3B-active MoE model served via Ollama, registered but not load-tested.
intention_for_embedding: Document why this quantized MoE was chosen for high-throughput and single-stream local chat use cases and what operational measurements remain to be done.
